title: Highest temperature in London on July 23?
description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the London City Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 23 Jul '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the London City Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/gb/london/EGLC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Forecast models from the Met Office and ECMWF currently indicate a broad high-pressure ridge over southern England through July 23, supporting daytime maxima centered in the mid-20s Celsius for London. Ensemble spreads produce closely matched probabilities for 24°C and 25°C because small differences in predicted cloud cover, boundary-layer mixing, and local wind speeds can shift the peak by 1–2°C. The urban heat-island effect adds further variability, with central stations often recording 1–3°C above rural sites under clear, light-wind conditions. Updated model runs expected in the next 24–48 hours will narrow the range ahead of market resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent model guidance shows a post-heatwave transition over western Europe, with an Atlantic ridge weakening and allowing cooler maritime air and increased instability to reach northern France by July 23. Ensemble spreads from ECMWF and GFS place daytime maxima near 25–27 °C, with small differences in cloud cover, timing of any showers, and boundary-layer mixing determining whether the peak reaches the upper or lower end of that range. Soil moisture from preceding storms and modest northerly flow further suppress extreme values, keeping probabilities tightly clustered around the 25–27 °C outcomes. Updated runs and morning soundings over the next 48 hours will likely tighten the distribution.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Incheon Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 23 Jul '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Incheon Intl Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/kr/incheon/RKSI.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Cloud cover and scattered rainfall tied to the East Asian summer monsoon are the main drivers keeping market-implied odds clustered around 25–29°C for Seoul’s July 23 maximum. Official guidance from the Korea Meteorological Administration highlights persistent clouds and showers that limit solar heating, while ensemble models show modest spread in afternoon instability and timing of any breaks. Typical July climatology places mean highs near 30–31°C, but enhanced moisture and reduced insolation this period favor slightly cooler peaks; outcomes above 30°C require clearer skies and stronger subsidence not currently favored in most runs. Traders weigh the latest model updates and observed trends in regional steering patterns ahead of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Forecast consensus from major models such as ECMWF and GFS places Ankara’s July 23 high near 29–30 °C under persistent high pressure and clear skies that favor strong daytime solar heating in the region’s continental climate. Small differences in boundary-layer mixing, wind speed, and exact cloud cover across ensemble members create the tight clustering around these values, with lower probabilities assigned to 28 °C or below and only minimal odds for 31 °C or higher. Historical July maxima in Ankara average near 31 °C, but recent model runs have trended slightly cooler, aligning with the balanced market-implied odds between 29 °C and 30 °C. Updated short-range guidance and any late adjustments in the 48-hour forecast window will be the next key inputs for traders.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus around 31–33°C for Hong Kong’s July 23 maximum reflects short-range forecast uncertainty driven by the interplay between subtropical high pressure and incoming tropical moisture. Updated HKO guidance and regional model ensembles expected within 24 hours will likely resolve these differences ahead of market resolution.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Singapore Changi Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 23 Jul '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Singapore Changi Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/sg/singapore/WSSS.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Singapore's equatorial climate keeps typical July daily maxima near 31–33°C under southwest monsoon flow, with afternoon thundery showers and variable cloud cover the main controls on peak temperature. Recent NEA guidance highlights localized convective activity that can cap highs near 31°C on overcast days while allowing brief clears to reach 32–34°C; any strengthening El Niño signal adds a modest warming bias through reduced rainfall. With only 48 hours until July 23, traders weigh the latest high-resolution model runs on boundary-layer moisture and timing of Sumatra squalls, which explain why implied odds cluster tightly around 30–32°C rather than extremes. Updated forecasts from MSS and global ensembles will likely shift probabilities before resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders see 30–32°C as the most likely range for Milan’s July 23 high because ensemble models show modest warm-air advection under a lingering subtropical ridge, tempered by partial Atlantic influence and variable cloud cover. Recent stabilization after the late-June heatwave has reduced extreme warming potential, while Po Valley trapping and Milan’s urban heat island add roughly 1–2 °C locally. Small differences among 30, 31, and 32 °C hinge on the exact timing of any weak trough, boundary-layer mixing depth, and afternoon insolation—variables that produce noticeable spread even 48 hours out. With probabilities tightly bunched, fresh model runs and updated observations on July 22 will likely refine the consensus before resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent ensemble forecasts from Taiwan's Central Weather Administration and global models show Taipei under typical midsummer subtropical high pressure on July 23, with daytime maxima most likely reaching 35–36 °C. Slight differences in predicted cloud cover, afternoon convection, and any peripheral moisture from distant tropical systems create the narrow spread between these outcomes that traders have priced near 50 % combined. eventMetadata.context_description:
**Market-implied odds cluster tightly around 28–31°C for Beijing’s highest temperature on July 23, 2026, reflecting forecast uncertainty in precipitation timing and cloud cover rather than a single dominant outcome.** Official guidance from sources such as the China Meteorological Administration and ensemble runs (ECMWF/GFS) points to highs near 29–31°C amid monsoon moisture and potential thundershowers, which limit surface heating through reduced insolation and evaporative cooling.

Key differentiating factors include the strength and timing of any frontal or convective activity: heavier or earlier rain favors the 28–29°C outcomes by suppressing peak insolation, while clearer intervals or delayed showers allow readings to reach 30–31°C. Humidity levels near 70–80% and light southerly flow further modulate the diurnal range, while Beijing’s urban heat island adds a localized boost of 1–2°C compared with rural surroundings. Model spread in the 48-hour window before the date drives the close probabilities, with new observational data and updated runs expected to narrow the range as July 23 approaches.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Wuhan Tianhe International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 23 Jul '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Wuhan Tianhe International Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/wuhan/ZHHH.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Short-range forecasts from agencies and models currently favor a July 23 high of 32–33°C in Wuhan, driving the tight clustering of market-implied probabilities around those outcomes. In this subtropical monsoon climate, the post-plum-rain transition brings variable steering flows, scattered thunderstorms, and high humidity that can suppress or enhance afternoon peaks by 1–2°C depending on cloud cover and timing of convection. Urban heat-island effects from the city’s numerous lakes and dense development further modulate readings at official stations, while ensemble spread in guidance creates the observed uncertainty between 31°C and 34°C. Historical July averages near 31–32°C provide baseline context, but traders weigh the latest model runs and potential for brief clear-sky intensification when assessing resolution thresholds.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the Chengdu July 23 high centers on 38–40 °C because recent ensemble forecasts from the ECMWF and GFS models show daytime maxima stabilizing in that narrow band under typical Sichuan Basin summer conditions. High humidity, weak steering flow, and urban heat-island effects support strong afternoon heating, while variable cloud cover and possible monsoon moisture intrusions introduce the main uncertainty that keeps adjacent outcomes within a few percentage points. Official guidance from the China Meteorological Administration will update overnight, with the next high-resolution runs expected to clarify whether subtle differences in boundary-layer moisture or insolation push the peak toward 39 °C or 40 °C. Historical July climatology for the region places the 90th-percentile daily maximum near 38 °C, underscoring why traders assign meaningful probability across this tight range.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Shenzhen Bao'an International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 23 Jul '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Shenzhen Bao'an International Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/shenzhen/ZGSZ.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent forecasts for Shenzhen point to a peak temperature in the low-to-mid 30s °C on July 23, driven by typical subtropical summer conditions with high humidity and potential for afternoon convection. Model consensus from sources like timeanddate and AccuWeather shows highs near 32–33 °C (roughly 90–92 °F), tempered by cloud cover and showers that can suppress the daily maximum. The tight spread across 31–34 °C outcomes reflects uncertainty in convective timing and any localized urban heat influences in the Pearl River Delta. Historical July averages of 32–33 °C provide context, while short-term variability from monsoon patterns keeps trader probabilities clustered rather than decisive. Updated regional model runs in the next 24–48 hours could refine the exact threshold.

eventMetadata.context_description: Forecast uncertainty in short-range numerical weather prediction models is the main driver behind the tight clustering of trader probabilities around 20–23°C for Moscow’s July 23 daily maximum. Ensemble spreads show modest variability in daytime heating tied to subtle differences in cloud cover, boundary-layer mixing, and weak cold-air advection ahead of a passing trough. Historical July climatology places the long-term average high near 23°C, yet current guidance from multiple centers indicates slightly suppressed values around 20–22°C due to increased cloudiness and lighter winds. Resolution hinges on the official Moscow station reading, where even a 1°C shift from model bias or local microclimate effects can separate the leading outcomes. Updated runs ahead of the 48-hour window will likely tighten or shift these probabilities as new observational data arrives.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Korea Meteorological Administration forecasts and model ensembles indicate mostly cloudy skies with scattered showers for Busan on July 23, moderating peak heating under typical East Asian summer monsoon conditions and coastal sea-breeze influence from the Korea Strait. This setup supports the market's tight clustering around 32–34°C as the most probable outcomes, with trader-implied odds reflecting uncertainty in precipitation timing and cloud cover. Brief clear intervals could permit greater insolation and push readings toward 34–35°C, while sustained showers or thicker cloud decks would favor 31°C or lower; historical July baselines near 29–31°C provide context for the current ensemble spread ahead of final observational data.

eventMetadata.context_description: **Forecast models indicate a mild day with highs likely peaking near 19–20°C in Amsterdam on July 23, as northwesterly flow and increasing cloud cover limit daytime heating.** Official guidance from sources like the BBC and European models shows highs around 19°C under light cloud and moderate breezes, following a similar 19°C reading on July 22 after a slightly warmer 21°C on the 21st. This aligns with mid-July climatology of 21–22°C but reflects cooler maritime air advection rather than any developing heat dome or subsidence. Key differentiators between the closely matched 18°C, 19°C, and 20°C outcomes include the precise timing and extent of cloud breaks, which control solar insolation, versus sustained northwest winds that enhance mixing and evaporative cooling. With resolution just 48 hours away, the next model runs and observational updates on boundary-layer conditions will determine whether modest clearing allows a brief spike toward 20°C or persistent cover caps the maximum closer to 18°C. Trader consensus reflects this narrow uncertainty range typical of short-term European summer forecasts.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for Kuala Lumpur’s July 23 daily high centers on the ongoing southwest monsoon and emerging El Niño conditions, which typically suppress rainfall while allowing afternoon temperatures to peak near the long-term July average of 32°C. Recent model guidance from agencies such as the Malaysian Meteorological Department indicates limited cloud cover and light winds over the next 48 hours, favoring highs of 32–33°C, consistent with the market’s leading shares at those levels. Urban heat-island effects in the Klang Valley can add 1–2°C above regional baselines on clear days, while any late-afternoon showers would cap readings closer to 31°C. Historical July maxima rarely exceed 34°C without strong radiative heating or stalled convection, keeping the 35°C+ tail probabilities modest. Updated short-range forecasts expected from the National Meteorological Centre within 24 hours will likely resolve remaining uncertainty between the tightly bunched 31–33°C outcomes.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent forecasts for Guangzhou indicate highs near 32–35°C on July 23, 2026, amid subtropical summer conditions with high humidity and convective activity typical of the East Asian monsoon transition. Model consensus points to variable cloud cover and scattered showers as the main differentiators, with clearer skies allowing greater solar insolation and surface heating to push maxima toward 35–36°C, while increased convection or lingering moisture caps temperatures nearer 33–34°C. Historical July averages hover around 33°C, but urban heat-island effects and weak steering winds can amplify daily peaks by 1–2°C when subsidence dominates. Upcoming model runs and satellite observations of moisture flux will refine these ranges ahead of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent summer conditions in coastal Qingdao feature typical July highs near 28–30 °C, moderated by Yellow Sea breezes that cap daytime peaks even during warm spells. With the July 23 maximum just two days ahead, trader odds cluster around 30–33 °C because ensemble forecasts show modest spread in cloud cover, humidity, and any late-arriving monsoon moisture that could suppress or allow a brief 31–32 °C reading. Clear, subsiding air under high pressure favors the upper end of the range, while scattered showers or stronger on-shore flow would keep readings nearer 29–30 °C. Official observations from the China Meteorological Administration will resolve the market on the daily maximum at the primary station, and the tight distribution of implied probabilities reflects genuine uncertainty in the final 48-hour model runs.

eventMetadata.context_description: Active southwest monsoon conditions and associated cloud cover plus scattered thunderstorms are the main drivers moderating Manila's daytime highs for July 23, with official PAGASA guidance and extended models indicating 31–33°C maxima amid 60% rain probabilities. In Manila's tropical monsoon climate, reduced solar insolation from convective clouds typically caps peaks 1–2°C below clear-sky potential, while high humidity and the urban heat island sustain baseline warmth near the 33°C July average. ENSO-neutral conditions following La Niña add little anomalous forcing this season. Recent 24–48 hour observations show similar partly cloudy patterns yielding 32–34°C readings, aligning with the market's tight clustering around 32–34°C outcomes and underscoring uncertainty from variable thunderstorm timing and intensity.

eventMetadata.context_description: Persistent high pressure and above-normal seasonal warmth across southeast Texas are anchoring trader expectations for Houston's July 23 high near the long-term July normal of 94–95°F, with the tight clustering around 90–95°F reflecting model consensus on limited moderation. Subsidence under the ridge promotes clear skies and strong daytime heating, yet modest differences in afternoon moisture convergence, sea-breeze timing, or scattered convection could trim or boost the peak by a degree or two. Recent NHC and NWS guidance shows heat-advisory conditions persisting through mid-week with minimal tropical influence, while ensemble spreads in short-range models highlight the narrow window separating the top three bins; fresh 00Z and 12Z runs plus any updated dew-point or boundary-layer forecasts will be the next key inputs for resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Short-term forecast uncertainty around the marine layer and onshore flow is the main driver behind the closely matched market odds favoring a San Francisco high of 68–71°F on July 23. National Weather Service guidance and ensemble models show modest afternoon warming as stratus clears, with sea-surface temperatures near seasonal norms and light winds limiting further rise above the upper 60s. Historical July maxima average near 69°F, but day-to-day variability from coastal eddy timing and inversion strength creates genuine spread across the 66–73°F bins. Updated model runs and afternoon observations tomorrow will refine the exact peak before resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent forecast model runs from agencies including Mexico's Servicio Meteorológico Nacional indicate peak temperatures in Mexico City tomorrow will likely reach 28–29°C, aligning with the market's leading implied probabilities near 72% combined. At 2,240 meters elevation, the city's highland location typically moderates extremes through cooler baseline air masses, though the ongoing summer rainy season brings variable cloud cover, humidity, and afternoon convection that can suppress or enhance daytime highs by 1–3°C. Recent observations show stable subtropical ridging with limited moisture advection, favoring modest warming relative to seasonal norms around 25–26°C. Traders appear to weigh ensemble consensus and historical analogs for late July, with resolution hinging on official maximum readings at the central observatory; any shift in overnight model guidance or unexpected clear-sky development could adjust these odds before close.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Marcos A. Gelabert Intl Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/pa/panama-city/MPMG.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest short-range forecast models from regional meteorological services indicate persistent tropical moisture and afternoon convection across Panama, moderating peak heating and supporting the market's tight clustering of implied probabilities around 28–30°C for July 23. In Panama City's July wet season, the Intertropical Convergence Zone typically drives scattered showers that reduce insolation after midday, keeping highs near the 29–30°C climatological average while limiting excursions above 31°C. Traders appear to weigh recent model runs showing variable cloud cover against historical analogs of 29°C means, with the narrow spread between leading outcomes reflecting genuine uncertainty in exact timing and intensity of convective cooling. Upcoming overnight model updates and local observations will likely refine these probabilities before resolution.
